description.value: Master hackers who put themselves at risk as they jack into cyberspace to download secrets and infiltrate networks.

information.notes:
Note: Kija is built with the NC G&C variant of Netrunner in mind - see journal entry 1b for details. While the RAW version of Netrunner can be used on its place, you'll need to re-purchase your gear to allow for programs, or else be prepared to face cyberspace naked.

Kija Choe is a Korean netrunner and immigrant who made the difficult transition to Night City as part of the terms of her twin sister's relocation for a corporate contract. Raised conservatively, exposure to this dramatically different way of life was initially a shock, but the Wanderer has nonetheless pressed forward into untangling the mysteries of the world's digital underbelly, so many of which seem centered upon her new home.

Kija was born in North Korea in the late 2020s, just before the Second Reunification War in the 2030s, and her family was selected to be uplifted from their impoverished conditions as part of a Sungan uplift program. In doing so, she (alongside her sister) studied art, science, self-defense, and information technology as part of the company's investment in a new generation of workers. Hana, her sibling, embraced the corporate line - but Kija was drawn to a different path.

She was always told that the decision which sent her parents back to their squalor in North Korea was a management error - one that emerged, coincidentally, after Sungan faced an incursion from beyond the Black Wall. But it was this first brush with AI that ignited her drive to understand it. What lies beyond the Black Wall? What secrets are still buried in the Old Net? What are the machines doing (and she is certain they are machines) in the ghost cities of Hong Kong and Busan? And what future are they planning, for themselves and for the rest of the world?

Difficulty: If playing with the streamlined, single-check NC G&C netrunning rules, easy. If playing with RAW netrunning, hard.

Style of Play: Stay out of the line of fire, and search for key weaknesses to be exploited by hacking machines, sabotaging key systems, procuring information or (GM permitting) even hacking enemy cyberware. Make scans of your area regularly, to see what's available to you.

Character Goals: Breach the Black Wall and discover the goals of the wild AIs beyond, and in so doing, find a greater understanding of the seeming chaos of the physical world.

Cyberware To Aspire To: An arm + an internal netdeck can fully disguise any outward appearance of a hacker, allowing for more discrete subterfuge. Tactile Boost will allow you to outwit opponents more easily. A cyberaudio suite + an internal agent and radio communicator will allow you to go full hands-free for your communications.

In Character Voice & Acting Inspiration: (in meatspace) Tali'Zorah, Mass Effect; (in digital space) Zer0, Borderlands 2; (in both) Sombra, Overwatch.

Local Expert: The Glen.

Science: Information Technology

Martial Art: Taekwondo (flying kick & pressure point strike - see CORE pg. 180)

lifepath.culturalOrigin: Born in North Korea shortly before the second reunification war, and relocated to Seoul in a first wave of immigration following CW4's conclusion.
lifepath.personality: Reserved and socially distant, but optimistic, thoughtful, and forward-thinking.
lifepath.clothingStyle: Korean leisurewear, typically more conservative. Almost always wears flowers - in print, on a piece of jewelry, in her hair. Wear a long dress on a typical day, and even in a firefight, wears a short skirt above pants.
lifepath.valueMost: Unraveling the secrets of a strange, isolated world.
lifepath.aboutPeople: Most are simple, many are fake. Very few are interesting, and yet, each is unique in their own special way.
lifepath.valuedPerson: Her twin sister, who works for the Night City office of Sungan Industries, the largest Korean megacorp.
lifepath.valuedPossession: A memory chip of strange, otherwise-worthless technical data found just beyond the Busan quarantine wall, which she believes proves the Ghost City Conspiracy.
lifepath.familyBackground: Parents were poor North Korean farmers, part of a very limited uplift program via Sungan Industries after the Second Reunification War in the early 2030s.
lifepath.childhoodEnvironment: Extremely strict, highly conservative parents ensured she was raised a quiet, proper young lady.
lifepath.familyCrisis: Despite doing everything right (or so they claimed), Kija's parents were let go from their highly-in-demand spaces in the Sungan Uplift, separating them from their daughters.
lifepath.lifeGoals: Breach the Blackwall and uncover the mysteries of what's REALLY happening in the world.
lifepath.friends: Hana Choe, her twin sister. The two are almost identical in appearance, but style themselves differently in terms of color choices, hairstyle, etc. Hana is the more outgoing of the two.
lifepath.enemies: Kija's repeated probing of the Black Wall has earned her the attention of Netwatch, who could potentially deal with her in any number of ways...
